steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to explain an advantage of Antelope House specifically. One note names the advantage: the overhanging rock ledges offered protection from heavy rain and snow. A correct answer must both identify this advantage and explain why it exists.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The relatively flat terrain on which Antelope House was built allowed for the construction of large terraced buildings.: Flat terrain and terraced buildings belong to Mud Springs Pueblo, not Antelope House. This swaps the two sites. ✗
  • Choice B — Located in northeastern Arizona, Antelope House is an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site that was inhabited from approximately 1050-1270 CE.: This states facts about Antelope House but names no advantage at all. ✗
  • Choice C — The location of Antelope House, an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site in northeastern Arizona, provided an advantage to its inhabitants.: This vaguely asserts an advantage exists but never identifies what it is or explains it. ✗
  • Choice D — Since it was built under a rock overhang, Antelope House was naturally protected from heavy rain and snow.: "Since" establishes the cause (built under a rock overhang) and the advantage (protection from rain and snow) together, which is exactly what the task requires. ✓
That matches Choice D — Since it was built under a rock overhang, Antelope House was naturally protected from heavy rain and snow..
